sanskrit: अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् । जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥
hindiTranslation: पृथ्वी, जल, तेज, वायु, आकाश  ये पञ्चमहाभूत और मन, बुद्धि तथा अहंकार  यह आठ प्रकारके भेदोंवाली मेरी अपरा प्रकृति है ।  हे महाबाहो  इस अपरा प्रकृतिसे भिन्न मेरी जीवरूपा बनी हुुई मेरी परा प्रकृतिको जान, जिसके द्वारा यह जगत् धारण किया जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Know My higher nature, which is different from this lower nature, O mighty-armed one. By that living nature, this world is sustained.
essence: What sustains the world is subtler than what the senses first show.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: There is a higher nature beyond matter and mind. That living power holds up the whole world.
simpleInsight: Reality is not just what changes. Something living and subtler is what keeps everything going.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen while Krishna begins explaining what lies behind the visible world. After naming the lower nature, Krishna points to a higher living nature that sustains everything. The teaching prepares Arjuna to see reality beyond what the senses can grasp.
modernRelevance: You look at a room full of charts, deadlines, and noise, and still feel something essential is missing. Krishna points to the deeper force that makes the whole system hold together.
translationLiteral: अपरा (lower) / इयम् (this) / इतः (from this) / तु (but) / अन्याम् (another) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / विद्धि (know) / मे (My) / पराम् (higher) / जीवभूताम् (become living) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / यया (by which) / इदम् (this) / धार्यते (is sustained) / जगत् (world)
insight: It shifts attention from surface appearances to the sustaining principle underneath them.
